label: Wide-shell 2-STOREY 3BR / 2 baths, REAR SIDEWAYS STAIR, hall-less ground floor, distributed baths (one per floor) — the first 2-storey 3BR. Ground floor: full-width great room across the front (entry), sideways 3.5 m stair + kitchen + T&B tiling the rear band. Upstairs: master and second bedroom street-facing at the front, third bedroom at the rear, and the landing hall as a compact mid-band CORE serving all five doors (three bedrooms, bath, stair arrival) — the 2-storey cousin of the 1s 3BR hall_core pinwheel. Canonical 12x10 lot (8x6 buildable per floor, 96 m² gross — mid-band for 3BR/2-bath 80-120 m²).

adjacencies.note: GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.
GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightline
GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom end
GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service door
VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)
2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wall
2F: master door off the landing hall
2F: second bedroom door off the landing hall
2F: third bedroom door off the landing hall-core
2F: second T&B doors off the landing hall

soft_proximities.note: 2F: pull the upper T&B toward the rear stair corner — lands it near the GF wet bay (kitchen + bath1 share the rear band), shortening the vertical plumbing run. Soft only: cross-floor wet-stack alignment isn't a hard v2 constraint yet.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: stair_run_geometry
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive — 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m; min_greatest_dim_m pins the run, max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight (orientation stays the solver's choice — it picks the sideways run on this shell). common_bath caps stop post-snap gap-filling from stretching a bath into a corridor. hallway max_area_sqm 6.0 (vs 4.5 on the 2BR siblings): this hall-core hosts FIVE doors — three bedrooms, the bath, and the stair arrival — so it earns more area, but stays bounded so snap growth returns spare m² to bedrooms instead.

notes: 2-STOREY wide catalog topology #2 — the FIRST 2-storey 3BR
(authored 2026-07-19; scaled up from 2s_2br_wd_rear_stair_bath_gr,
keeping its sideways rear stair and full-width front great room).
Program: 3BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED one per floor (baths_ds) — GF common
T&B off the great room, upper common bath off the landing hall. All
three bedrooms upstairs. Bath count matches the locked 3BR/2-bath
floor band (80-120 m² gross).
Why the hall is a CORE, not a corridor: putting all three bedrooms
across the front (the tempting wide move) forces a back corridor
spanning all three door walls — ~6.5 m of hallway, past the 4:1
hallway aspect cap and against the minimal-notch convention. Instead
master + br2 take the street front, br3 takes the rear beside the
bath, and the hall sits as a compact mid-band node (~5-6 m²) with all
five doors radiating from it — the 2-storey cousin of the 1s
1s_3br_sq_hall_core pinwheel.
Canonical layout (12 x 10 lot, 2 m setbacks, 8 x 6 buildable/floor):
GF:  Front: [great room (entry, FULL width, counter at rear edge)]
Rear:  [stair (sideways) -> | kitchen | T&B                 ]
2F:  Front: [br2            | master                             ]
Mid:   [hall-core (5 doors)         | br3                   ]
Rear:  [stairwell      | T&B        | br3                   ]
Stair: straight, 0.9-1.2 m wide, >= 3.5 m sideways run, 15 risers @
200 mm / 250 mm treads, left-anchored; boards off the great room,
arrives at the hall-core (stair_boarding / stair_arrival end
constraints, horizontal-run branch).
Verified sizes (all COMPLIANT, 0 warnings, deterministic): 12x10
canonical (96 m² gross), 13x10 (108), 11.5x9.5 (82 — band floor),
14x10.5 (130 — above band, solver-fine, offer 4BR instead per the
floor-band convention). Technically solves down to 11x9 (70 m²
gross) — BELOW the 3BR band floor; treat the 80 m² band, not solver
feasibility, as the practical minimum when matching briefs.
Structure/override choices: deliberately minimal — only the stair is
anchored (single-member left_anchored) and only two gentle stacks
([master, hall2], [br2, hall2]) pull those two bedrooms in front of
the hall; br3/bath2/stairwell arrange freely via adjacencies (no
zone_split — br3 belongs in the rear band, unlike the 2BR siblings
whose split pins both bedrooms front). zone_balance_rooms counts the
whole upper floor minus stairwell as private. Kitchen mechanical_vent
per the wide-2BR rationale (counter-width, service-door-dominated rear
wall at compact sizes). Soft proximity bath2->stairwell keeps the
upper bath near the GF wet bay (cross-floor wet-stack alignment is
soft-only in v2).
Known quirk (shared with the 2BR wide sibling): when the solver floats
the sideways flight off the rear wall, the strip behind it is claimed
as an alcove where an eligible room abuts it, or stays a small
suggestion-level hole where only baths/stairs adjoin (both are
excluded claimants) — honest behind-stair space either way.
Naming: `baths_ds` = distributed baths (one per floor), `gr` = open
great-room style, no `_hall` token since the GROUND floor is hall-less
(the 2F landing hall is mandatory in every 2s topology and is not
tokened, consistent with the 2s 2BR family).
